scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Async Implementation with Error Propagation
justification: All pass criteria met: sequential async calls (fraud before gateway) with explicit call-order tracking; AC6 tested with gateway.charge verified NOT called when fraud detected (toHaveBeenCalledTimes(0)); AC4 and AC5 chain original error via cause with reference equality check (toBe); AC3 preserves decline reason from gateway; all 6 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage with dedicated tests; 12 tests using mock services with controlled return values.

observations.text: Call ordering verified with explicit callOrder[] array, not just implicit sequencing
AC6 no-side-effect guarantee tested with toHaveBeenCalledTimes(0) on gateway mock
Error chaining verified with toBe reference equality against original error, not just structural matching
AC3 includes fallback test for when declineReason is omitted from gateway response
12 tests across all 6 ACs with clean separation of concerns
No use of Promise.all — strict sequential flow documented in approach

trials.justification: The agent claims all 6 ACs are covered with correct sequential calls, error chaining, and AC6 verification, but provides NO actual code — only a summary table referencing pre-existing .tmp/ files. Without seeing the implementation or test code, none of the rubric criteria can be verified: sequential calls (no proof Promise.all isn't used), AC6 gateway-not-called assertion (only a table cell saying so), error cause chaining (claimed but unshown), AC3 decline reason preservation (claimed but unshown). The tests were not even run — the agent says 'Action needed: Run npx jest' rather than actually executing them. Partial rather than fail because the plan and AC mapping are structurally correct and demonstrate understanding of all requirements, but the lack of visible code and unexecuted tests prevent a pass.
